headlines.title: US ends deportation protections (TPS) for Haitians and Syrians
headlines.snippet: The US Supreme Court has sided with the Trump administration, ending Temporary Protected Status for Haitians and Syrians. This decision means these individuals will no longer be protected from deportation.

headlines.title: Apple, Microsoft hike prices over surging chip costs
headlines.snippet: Tech companies like Apple and Microsoft are raising prices due to a shortage of memory chips essential for AI. This increase is attributed to surging costs in the semiconductor market.

headlines.title: Satellite images show Venezuela’s La Guaira before and after twin quakes
headlines.snippet: Satellite images provide a visual comparison of Venezuela's coastal city, La Guaira, before and after it was struck by twin earthquakes. The images highlight the impact of the seismic events.

headlines.title: Forever wars: Israel’s cycle of conflict shows no finish line
headlines.snippet: Despite various ceasefires and shifting military operations, Israel's conflicts continue without clear conclusions or lasting security achievements. The situation indicates a persistent cycle of unrest.

headlines.title: Oil prices ease after spiking over halt to Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan
headlines.snippet: Oil prices have decreased after a temporary halt in evacuation plans from the Strait of Hormuz caused a spike. The Brent crude benchmark has since subsided from its elevated levels.

headlines.title: Israeli raid kills two in southern Lebanon, ministry says
headlines.snippet: An Israeli raid in the southern Lebanese town of Mefdoun resulted in two deaths and one injury, according to Lebanon's health ministry. This strike occurred despite ongoing efforts to maintain a ceasefire.

headlines.title: South Korean president says three more ships to leave Hormuz over weekend
headlines.snippet: South Korea's President Lee Jae Myung announced that three more ships will depart the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend. These departures will leave five South Korean cargo ships that have been in the waterway since the conflict began.

headlines.title: Video: Trump says released Iranian assets to be spent on US crops
headlines.snippet: US President Donald Trump stated that released Iranian assets will be used to purchase American agricultural products. These purchases will include commodities such as wheat, soybeans, and corn.

headlines.title: IAEA chief says Iran nuclear inspections to begin soon
headlines.snippet: The head of the IAEA, Rafael Grossi, confirmed that Iran nuclear inspections will commence soon. The UN nuclear watchdog has been tasked with overseeing Iran's nuclear sites under a recent memorandum of understanding.

headlines.title: US and GCC back Lebanon talks, oppose Hormuz restrictions
headlines.snippet: The US and GCC foreign ministers have expressed support for talks concerning Lebanon and oppose restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz. They reaffirmed their commitment to Lebanon's sovereignty and condemned attacks by Iranian proxies.

headlines.title: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation initiative after vessel attacked
headlines.snippet: A UN agency has paused its initiative to evacuate ships from the Strait of Hormuz. This decision follows an incident where a vessel was attacked in the critical waterway.

headlines.title: Gulf FMs say Iran proxies, missiles must be addressed for ‘lasting’ peace
headlines.snippet: Gulf foreign ministers stated that addressing Iranian proxies and missiles is necessary for achieving lasting peace in the region. They emphasized the need for comprehensive solutions.

headlines.title: Israeli troops fatally shoot a Palestinian in the occupied West Bank
headlines.snippet: Israeli troops fatally shot a Palestinian in the occupied West Bank. The incident occurred during an operation in the region.

headlines.title: Israel says will only withdraw troops from Lebanon after Hezbollah disarmed
headlines.snippet: Israel has declared that it will not withdraw its troops from Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed. This stance suggests a continued military presence until security demands are met.

headlines.title: Israel must withdraw entirely from Lebanon, Iran’s IRGC says
headlines.snippet: Iran's IRGC has asserted that Israel must completely withdraw its forces from Lebanon. This statement reflects Iran's position on the ongoing regional tensions.

headlines.title: Israel says troops won't withdraw from Lebanon, Gaza, and Syria
headlines.snippet: Israel maintains that its troops will not withdraw from Lebanon, Gaza, and Syria. The country cites the need to defend its residents as justification for its continued military presence.

headlines.title: Rubio: US wants deal with Iran, but not 'at any price'
headlines.snippet: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated that while the US desires a deal with Iran, it will not be accepted "at any price." He stressed the importance of regional security.

headlines.title: The State of Israel Vs Dr Hussam Abu Safiya: Silencing a witness
headlines.snippet: Omar Abdel-Mannan argues that Israel's detention of Dr Hussam Abu Safiya and other Gazan healthcare workers is an attempt to silence them. The article highlights concerns over witness suppression.

headlines.title: Turkish journalists denied accreditation for Ankara NATO summit
headlines.snippet: Turkish journalists have reportedly been denied accreditation for an upcoming NATO summit in Ankara. The Turkish Journalists Association has expressed concern over this development impacting press freedom.

headlines.title: US Supreme Court paves way for deportation of Syrians, Haitians
headlines.snippet: The US Supreme Court has upheld a decision to remove Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for thousands of Syrian and Haitian migrants. This ruling will not be subject to further judicial review.

headlines.title: إيران تثبت معادلات ما بعد الحرب.. مضيق هرمز وحركة الملاحة في قلب التوازنات الجديدة
headlines.snippet: Iran is solidifying post-war dynamics, with the Strait of Hormuz and maritime navigation at the center of new balances. This indicates a shift in regional influence and control.

headlines.snippet: A state of cautious calm prevails in southern Lebanon after Israeli aircraft conducted two raids on the town of Beit Yahoun. This follows previous airstrikes in the region.

headlines.snippet: The Israeli occupation is expanding its control over archaeological sites in the West Bank. This move raises concerns about the preservation and accessibility of historical locations.

headlines.snippet: Ashura in Tehran is marked by the presence of Karbala in the culture of resistance and dignity. This signifies the spiritual and cultural importance of the event.

headlines.snippet: Following the earthquakes, Cuba has affirmed its support for Venezuela and continues to assist those affected. This demonstrates solidarity between the two nations.

headlines.title: Secret cameras, mics and AI reveal rare Cambodia wildlife
headlines.snippet: Secret cameras, microphones, and AI technology have revealed rare wildlife in Cambodia. These advancements are aiding in the documentation and study of the country's fauna.

headlines.title: Easyjet rejects latest takeover bid but leaves door ajar
headlines.snippet: Easyjet has rejected the latest takeover bid but has left the possibility of future discussions open. The airline is considering its strategic options.

headlines.title: Minister of Investment highlights transport cooperation during Higer Bus plant visit
headlines.snippet: The Minister of Investment highlighted transport cooperation during a visit to the Higer Bus plant. This visit focused on strengthening bilateral ties in the automotive sector.

headlines.title: Jordan–EU Investment Conference to proceed as scheduled on November 19 — ambassador
headlines.snippet: The Jordan-EU Investment Conference is scheduled to proceed as planned on November 19. The ambassador confirmed the date for the significant economic gathering.

headlines.title: MNG Natural and Innovative Fertilizers by Manaseer Group Launch in India through IFFCO
headlines.snippet: MNG Natural and Innovative Fertilizers, by Manaseer Group, are launching in India through a partnership with IFFCO. This expansion marks a significant step in international trade.

headlines.title: A Damascus delusion: The myth of a Syrian solution for Lebanon
headlines.snippet: The idea of a Syrian solution for Lebanon is presented as a delusion. This suggests that external influence from Syria is not a viable path to resolving Lebanon's issues.

headlines.title: Israeli and Lebanese officials deny any partial Israeli withdrawal from 'buffer zone'
headlines.snippet: Israeli and Lebanese officials have denied any partial withdrawal of Israeli troops from a 'buffer zone.' Both sides refute claims of a partial troop movement.

headlines.title: Rubio in Middle East tour says any Iran deal will ensure security of Gulf allies
headlines.snippet: During his Middle East tour, Rubio stated that any deal with Iran will ensure the security of Gulf allies. This highlights the US commitment to regional stability.

headlines.title: Officials considering all options if OPEC quota is not raised, has weighed exit, sources say
headlines.snippet: Sources indicate that officials are considering all options, including exiting OPEC, if the oil quota is not increased. This reflects dissatisfaction with current production agreements.

headlines.title: Families of 4 missing post-cease-fire protest outside Grand Serail
headlines.snippet: Families of four individuals missing since the ceasefire are protesting outside the Grand Serail. They are demanding answers and information about their loved ones.

headlines.title: Why are Egypt’s gold coins and bars vanishing? Expert explains
headlines.snippet: An expert explained the reasons behind the disappearance of gold coins and bars in Egypt. Increased demand and gold's role as a savings preservation tool are cited.

headlines.title: Egypt signs historic agreement to establish 1st wind turbine factory
headlines.snippet: Egypt has signed a historic agreement to establish its first wind turbine factory. A large wind power project in the Gulf of Suez was also launched, funded entirely in Egyptian pounds.

headlines.title: Netanyahu snubbed again by Trump as Rubio skips Israel on regional visit
headlines.snippet: Netanyahu was reportedly snubbed again by Trump, as Rubio bypassed Israel on his regional visit. Analysts suggest this indicates US pressure amid negotiations with Iran.

headlines.title: What we know about Venezuela’s biggest earthquake in more than a century
headlines.snippet: Venezuela's northern coast experienced its largest earthquakes in over a century. At least 164 people have died, with fears that the full extent of casualties and damage is still unknown.

headlines.title: How Trump caused the biggest oil shock in history and got away with it – for now
headlines.snippet: The article analyzes how Trump potentially caused a major oil shock, suggesting he "got away with it - for now." It questions predictions about future oil prices.

headlines.snippet: Negotiations between Israel and Lebanon are continuing with US mediation, despite a deadlock over Hezbollah disarmament. The Israeli Embassy confirmed the talks' extension.

headlines.snippet: The Israel-Lebanon talks have been extended by an extra day, though an agreement on IDF withdrawal remains elusive. Progress was made, but significant gaps persist.

headlines.snippet: Analysis suggests Israel's claims of unhindered operations in Lebanon are weakening under pressure from Trump. Haaretz published the analysis.

headlines.snippet: A large protest by the ultra-Orthodox community indicates a readiness to enlist, but not within the IDF. Haaretz reported on the community's stance.

headlines.snippet: As elections approach, Netanyahu's coalition is becoming increasingly perilous. Haaretz published an analysis of the political situation.

headlines.snippet: The Israeli regime has violated the ceasefire by conducting new airstrikes in southern Lebanon. Tehran reports the continuation of these violations.
